message:
fix(tests): clippy lint compliance across harness and e2e test suites

Mechanical lint fixes across 26 harness test files, 2 harness library
modules, and 1 e2e test file. All changes are behavior-preserving.

Vectorized benchmark test (bd_14vp7_7):
- Add module-level #![allow(...)] for cast lints inherent to the
  benchmark's integer↔float data manipulation
- Hoist `use std::collections::HashMap` to module level (was duplicated
  in two function bodies)
- Replace match-with-continue patterns with let...else for SqliteValue
  destructuring in interpreter_q1 and interpreter_q6
- Replace manual range check with .contains() for discount bounds
- Replace `sel.len() > 0` with `!sel.is_empty()`
- Add #[allow(clippy::type_complexity)] on interpreter_q1 return type

Left-right metadata test (bd_2bg7t):
- Rename `writes` variable to `write_count` to avoid shadowing the
  `writes` method on LeftRight (clippy::similar_names)
- Add #[allow(clippy::needless_collect)] on pair/triple consistency tests
  where collecting into Vec is required for thread join handles
- Add #[allow(clippy::cast_possible_truncation)] on latency benchmark

Harness infrastructure (bloodstream.rs, fault_vfs.rs):
- Derive Eq on PropagationConfig and PropagationMetrics
- Use Self:: instead of type name in const array declarations
- Merge match arms with shared fields using | pattern
- Add targeted lint suppressions on metrics functions with inherent casts
- Add #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)] on check_write and
  verify_metrics_contract

Across remaining test files:
- Add #[allow(dead_code)] on unused helper functions retained for
  future test expansion (tpch_indexes, row_values)
- Minor variable renames to avoid similar_names warnings
- Add crate-level lint allows for test-pattern noise
- Add #[allow(clippy::type_complexity)] on complex return types in
  SSI serialization correctness test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

     result

     rng: &mut StdRng,
